flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.
Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

attacks.name: Crystal Breath
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It spits out cold air of nearly -60 degrees Fahrenheit to freeze its quarry. It brings frozen prey back to its lair and neatly lines them up.

rules:
You can play this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand. Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

You can play this card only if any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn. Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

Draw 2 cards. If you do, your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Nitro Dive
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Bird Pokémon make up most of its diet. It approaches at high speeds and smacks them down to the ground with its powerful kick.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its speed right after takeoff already puts it in the top speed class of all bird Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tailwind Draw
attacks.effect: Draw a card. If you go second and it's your first turn, draw 3 more cards.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its body is always warm. Trainers who live in cold areas apparently sleep with it in their bed.

attacks.name: Valiant Talons
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Brave Bird
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Because this Pokémon is hotheaded and belligerent, it's Corviknight that's taken the role of transportation in Galar.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A combative Pokémon, it's ready to pick a fight with anyone. It has talons that can crush hard berries.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: Its fur is long and thick. A long time ago in cold regions, every household kept a Stoutland.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has been living with people for so long that portrayals of it can be found on the walls of caves from long, long ago.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is popular with beginners because it's intelligent, obedient to its Trainer's commands, and easy to raise.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

attacks.name: Rout
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is the world's most slothful Pokémon. However, it can exert horrifying power by releasing pent-up energy all at once.

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play.

flavorText: Its stress level rises if it cannot keep moving constantly. Too much stress makes it feel sick.

attacks.name: Smack 'n' Slack
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: If it eats just three leaves in a day, it is satisfied. Other than that, it sleeps for 20 hours a day.

attacks.name: Live Painting
attacks.effect: Reveal any number of basic Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 more damage for each type of basic Energy you revealed in this way.

flavorText: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.
Although it has a large body, it is quite skilled at climbing trees. It eats and sleeps in the treetops.
Before food becomes scarce in wintertime, its habit is to hoard food in many hidden locations.

attacks.name: Vee-Search
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

attacks.name: Dragon Energy
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.

attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

attacks.name: Fighting Tackle
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

flavorText: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

attacks.name: Extreme Freeze
attacks.effect: Discard any amount of Water Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

attacks.name: Dragon Counter
attacks.effect: This attack does 100 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took during their last turn.

flavorText: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won't slow until their target goes down.
Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Because it can't see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.

attacks.name: Chrono Wind
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

attacks.name: Max Burst
attacks.effect: You may discard any amount of basic Fire Energy or any amount of basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

attacks.name: Spiral Burst
attacks.effect: You may discard up to 2 basic Fire Energy or up to 2 basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 80 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fierce Dragon
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Thanks to its fervent wishes, the cells in its body finally mutated, and at last it has its heart's desire—wings.

attacks.name: Hard Roll
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Shelgon lives deep within caves. It stays shut up in its hard shell, dreaming of the day it will be able to fly.
Some theories suggest that its behavior of forcefully bashing its head into things stimulates cells that affect its evolution.
On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.
With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.
Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.
If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.
If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.

attacks.name: G-Max Malodor
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Trash Stench
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shakedown
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

attacks.name: Hard Head
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 10 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It protects itself with its durable skin. It's thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

attacks.name: Shiftadieu
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any damage counters on it, put it and all attached cards into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Nipping Cyclone
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf's grass flute fills listeners with dread.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Moonlight Blade
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fiery Wrath
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

flavorText: The sinister aura that blazes like molten fire around this Pokémon is what inspired the name Moltres.

attacks.name: Hunting Claw
attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play that has 60 HP or less remaining.

attacks.name: Max Edge
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crashing Fangs
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shaky Wave
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.
On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 50 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Pressure Shot
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

flavorText: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It relies on sound in order to monitor what's in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it's facing.
When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

attacks.name: Sand Press
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When roused to violence by its rage, it spews out the quantities of sand it has swallowed and whips up a sandstorm.
This Pokémon is active during the day and passes the cold desert nights burrowed snugly into the sand.

attacks.name: Yoga Loop
attacks.effect: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take another turn after this one. (Skip Pokémon Checkup.) If 1 of your Pokémon used Yoga Loop during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

attacks.name: Smash Uppercut
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zapper Kick
attacks.effect: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: One kick from its powerful legs will pulverize a dump truck. Supposedly, this Pokémon runs through the mountains at over 180 mph.

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Bullet Straight Punch
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

attacks.name: Rapid Hunt
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Rapid Strike cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Shadow Flicker
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out during your next turn, take 1 more Prize card.

flavorText: It sinks into the shadows of people and Pokémon, where it can understand their feelings and copy their capabilities.

attacks.name: Tricky Steps
attacks.effect: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.
An opponent's aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent's next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

attacks.name: Pandemonium
attacks.effect: Reveal the top 6 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 damage for each Psychic Pokémon you find there. Then, shuffle those Pokémon back into your deck and discard the other cards.

flavorText: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.
The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

attacks.name: Precious Touch
attacks.effect: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Harmony
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wonder Shine
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its life can span several hundred years. It's said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it's collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

attacks.name: Rewind Beam
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an evolved Pokémon, devolve it by putting the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Synchro Woofer
attacks.effect: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon's nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

flavorText: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

attacks.name: Go and Collect
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Corner
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It feeds on gemstone crystals. In darkness, its eyes sparkle with the glitter of jewels.

attacks.name: Mirror Pain
attacks.effect: Put damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon equal to the number of damage counters on 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.

attacks.name: Max Mindstorm
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Zen Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon V. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Psylaser
attacks.effect: Discard all Psychic Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Known as Articuno, this Pokémon fires beams that can immobilize opponents as if they had been frozen solid.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Wake-Up Slap
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 90 more damage. Then, that Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.

flavorText: There are some Hypno that assist doctors with patients who can't sleep at night in hospitals.
It puts its prey to sleep and devours their dreams. It seems that bad dreams taste sour, so Drowzee doesn't particularly like eating them.

attacks.name: Teraspark
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack also does 40 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar's electricity.

attacks.name: Spark Trap
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if it is Knocked Out), put 12 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Primeval Beak
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, Energy cards can't be attached from your opponent's hand to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mountain Swing
attacks.effect: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As it flies, it scatters electricity around, so bird Pokémon keep their distance. That's why Emolga can keep all its food to itself.

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electron Crush
attacks.effect: You may discard 3 Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.
It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.
Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

attacks.name: Blinding Beam
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.
On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

attacks.name: Max Thunder Rumble
attacks.effect: This attack also does 100 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Big Sparking
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to each Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its electric pouches run empty, it raises its tail to gather electricity from the atmosphere.

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.effect: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When Pikachu meet, they'll touch their tails together and exchange electricity through them as a form of greeting.

attacks.name: Ancient Freeze
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon V or a Pokémon-GX, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Giga Impact
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Blockface
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

attacks.name: Schooling Shot
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each basic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

attacks.name: Frost Barrier
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: As Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon's body returns to a pristine state overnight.
This Pokémon lives in areas of frigid cold. It secures itself to the back of an Avalugg by freezing its feet in place.

attacks.name: Element Chain
attacks.effect: Look at the top 6 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: They are composed of ice crystals. They capture prey with chains of ice, freezing the prey at -148 degrees Fahrenheit.
It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can't hear its cries of warning.

attacks.name: Max Icicle
attacks.effect: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Awakening
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Heavy Snow
attacks.effect: Discard a Stadium in play.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Synchrodraw
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola's branches.

attacks.name: Double Smash
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's said that a glimpse of a Milotic and its beauty will calm any hostile emotions you're feeling.

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Jet Bite
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It's very sensitive to the scent of blood.
With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.
If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.
It lives at the water's edge where it is sunny. It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and becomes active at night.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its leaf grew too large for it to live on land. That is how it began to live floating in the water.

attacks.name: Blizzard Rondo
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Pod
attacks.effect: Put a Water Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench. If you do, attach up to 3 Water Energy cards from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Max Torrent
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Get Angry
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poisonous Prison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: It communicates with others of its kind by lighting up the red orbs on its head. When the orbs are blinking, it's a warning sign.
Its body is 99% water. The remaining 1% contains the organ that makes its poison.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A professional swimmer, it can continue swimming for two days straight by waving its long tail skillfully.
It has been found that its brain cells are 10 times more active when Psyduck is experiencing a headache.

attacks.name: Rip Claw
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn't use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.
This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

attacks.name: Surging Flames
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each basic Energy card in your discard pile. Then, shuffle those Energy cards into your deck.

attacks.name: Fire Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Victory Dive
attacks.effect: You may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being's entire body will be overflowing with power.

attacks.name: Angry Fang
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Single Strike Pokémon.

attacks.name: Heat Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

attacks.name: Max Detonate
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 100 damage for each Energy card you discarded in this way.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

flavorText: As soon as it's born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

attacks.name: Cotton Guard
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

attacks.name: Blot
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Missing in the Forest
attacks.effect: This attack does 40 damage for each Supporter card in your opponent's discard pile.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shadow Claw
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They're mighty enough to crack Rhyperior's carapace.

attacks.name: Sharp Claws
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

attacks.name: Dizzying Flower
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It's well liked by other Pokémon because of its beauty. The flower on its head needs constant care, or it will soon wither and rot.
They prefer clean water and soil. When the environment they live in turns bad, the whole bunch will up and move to a new area.

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Max Leaf
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 more damage.

rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rally Back
attacks.effect: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

flavorText: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.
Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.
It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

attacks.name: Continuous Spin
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.
Although it's tough, it can't handle cold well. When night falls, it buries itself in leafage and sleeps.
